# Diego Sánchez (CuB1z) Portfolio

Diego Sánchez is a Software Engineer based in Madrid, Spain, focused on software
architecture, distributed systems and cloud. Recently graduated in Software
Engineering at Universidad Rey Juan Carlos (URJC); currently a Full-Stack
Developer at Génexys, building microservices with Python, FastAPI and AWS.

This is a bilingual single-page portfolio plus a blog. English is served at the
root; Spanish under /es.

## Navigation

- / (Home): hero, experience, selected work and about, on one page. Spanish: /es.
- /blog (Writing): posts on engineering, architecture and the TripFlow project. Spanish: /es/blog.
- /blog/<slug>: an individual blog post. Spanish: /es/blog/<slug>.

## Blog posts

Newest first. Spanish translations live under /es/blog/<slug>.

- Introducing TripFlow: a travel-planning PWA with AI itineraries and route optimization. https://cub1z.es/blog/introducing-tripflow
- CI/CD & Testing TripFlow: backend and frontend setup, test suites and deployment pipelines. https://cub1z.es/blog/ci-cd-testing-tripflow
- TripFlow MVP: authentication, itinerary management and a Dockerized, fully tested app. https://cub1z.es/blog/tripflow-mvp
- TripFlow v0.2.0 (AI & Microservices): AI itinerary generation and a Kafka-based microservices architecture. https://cub1z.es/blog/tripflow-v0.2.0
- Beyond the Monolith: when to adopt microservices versus keeping a modular monolith. https://cub1z.es/blog/beyond-the-monolith
- YAGNI in Practice: avoiding speculative over-engineering and the maintenance tax it creates. https://cub1z.es/blog/yagni-in-practice
- TripFlow v1.0.0: collaborative itineraries, secure share links, maps, offline access and PDF export. https://cub1z.es/blog/tripflow-v1.0.0

## Experience

- Génexys Technology, Full-Stack Developer (Feb 2026 – present): microservices with Python, FastAPI and AWS.
- Telefónica Innovación Digital, Full-Stack Developer, internship (Jul – Dec 2025): Next.js, accessibility (WCAG), SEO and web performance.

## Featured project

- TripFlow, Bachelor's Thesis (10/10): an AI-assisted travel planner with real-time collaboration and an offline mode, on a Spring Boot microservices backend with a React frontend. Live at https://tripflow.cub1z.es

## Technical skills

- Backend: Python, FastAPI, Java, Spring Boot, Node.js, microservices, REST APIs
- Frontend: React, Next.js, Astro, TypeScript, JavaScript
- Cloud & tooling: AWS, Docker, Git, CI/CD
- Focus: software architecture, distributed systems, cloud-native
